title: High-throughput DNA extraction for amplicon sequencing of infected plant samples
author: Sophia Haeussler

steps.title: Collect infected leaf material and dry
steps.instruction: Collect infected leaf material and dry.

steps.title: Prepare a 96 collection tube plate (e
steps.instruction: Prepare a 96 collection tube plate (e.g. Quiagen 19560) and fill with clean, Ethanol-washed 4 mm steel beads, 1 bead per tube. Note: Tube holders can be reused and refilled with 8-strips (we used 8-strip collection tubes, Brand, 781525 with lids, Brand, 781535).

steps.title: Cut out 2-3 lesions per leaf...
steps.instruction: Cut out 2-3 lesions per leaf (2-3 cm in total) using sterilized scissors, cut into 0.5 cm pieces and place in tube. Avoid cross-contamination between samples when placing samples into 96-tube plate. This can be achieved by taping wells with laboratory tape.

steps.title: Close tightly with corresponding lids (19566,...
steps.instruction: Close tightly with corresponding lids (19566, Quiagen for Quiagen 8-strips; 781535, Brand, for Brand 8-strips).

steps.title: Freeze the sample in liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: Freeze the sample in liquid nitrogen. Grind the sample using a ball mill (we used TissueLyser II, Quiagen) at 20 Hz for 1 min. Freeze again in liquid nitrogen and repeat grinding. The right combination of lids and strips is important to avoid opening of lids and cross-contamination during the grinding process. In addition, a quick spin-down after grinding helps to remove the material from the upper part of the tubes which reduces the risk of carryover when opening the tubes later.

steps.title: Store samples at -80 °C or proceed immediately
steps.instruction: Store samples at -80 °C or proceed immediately.

steps.title: Preheat water bath to 60 °C
steps.instruction: Preheat water bath to 60 °C. Add RNase A (3 µl/600 µl volume) to extraction buffer. When samples were stored at -80 °C prior to extraction, we recommend taking them out a few minutes before starting as lids stick to the tubes very tightly when frozen. When thawed a bit, the lids can be removed more easily and do not break. We used Purelink RNase A (Invitrogen 12091039; Stock concentration 20 mg/ml).

steps.title: Add 600 µl extraction buffer with...
steps.instruction: Add 600 µl extraction buffer with RNase A to each well. Remove liquid from the top of the tubes with tissue. Close the plate with sticky aluminium foil and vortex thoroughly.

steps.title: Incubate in 60 °C water bath...
steps.instruction: Incubate in 60 °C water bath for at least 10 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 6200 g for 15 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 6200 g for 15 min. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6200 g, centrifugation at 4000 g for 25 minutes can be performed instead.

steps.title: Transfer 350 µl of the clear...
steps.instruction: Transfer 350 µl of the clear supernatant to 2 ml deep well plate (e.g. Axygen P-DW-20-C). When using an electrical multichannel pipette, we recommend using setting “manual pipet” at speed 1.

steps.title: Add 1x volume of 96 %...
steps.instruction: Add 1x volume of 96 % Ethanol (350 µl) and mix by pipetting. When using an electrical multichannel pipette, we recommend using setting “pipet/mix” at speed 5, mixing volume 500 µl, mix cycles 6.

steps.title: Transfer 700 µl to an Econospin...
steps.instruction: Transfer 700 µl to an Econospin 96 well filter plate (2020-001, EpoChlifescience). Place filterplate on top of the used 2 ml deep well plate. The protocol has also successfully been tested with the AHN myPlate Microfilter Plate.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 6200 g for 2 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 6200 g for 2 min. If the centrifuge cannot reach 6200 g, centrifugation at 4000 g for 5 minutes can be performed as an alternative. This also applies to all following centrifugation steps.

steps.title: Add 450 µl 70 % Ethanol. Optional
steps.instruction: Add 450 µl 70 % Ethanol. Optional: Incubate for 5 min, especially if columns are clogged. Centrifuge at 6200 g for 2 min.

steps.title: Add 350 µl 70 % Ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 350 µl 70 % Ethanol. Centrifuge at 6200 g for 2 min. If the DNA samples contain any contamination, you can increase the volume of 70 % Ethanol to 700 µl or repeat this wash step.

steps.title: Discard flowthrough
steps.instruction: Discard flowthrough. Place Econospin 96 well filter plate on top of the 2 ml deep well plate and dry spin at 6200 g or 4000 g for 4 min.

steps.title: Place Econospin 96 well filter plate...
steps.instruction: Place Econospin 96 well filter plate on top of a 96 well PCR plate. For stabilization during centrifugation, put this PCR plate into at 1.1 ml deepwell plate (e.g. Axygen P-DW-11-C).

steps.title: Add 40 µl MilliQ water (optional
steps.instruction: Add 40 µl MilliQ water (optional: preheated to 55 °C). Incubate for 2 min. Centrifuge at 6200 g for 1 min. The expected yield should be in the range of 200 – 500 ng/µl. The amount of MilliQ water added to the columns can be adapted according to specific needs: To maximize total yield, increase the volume to 80 µl. To maximize concentration, use 40 µl.
